title: White bread rolls
subtitle: Plain white rolls with a good crust
excerpt: Plain white rolls with a thin, crisp crust and a light, open crumb. These are the bread-roll baseline: no enrichment, no flavouring, just flour, water, yeast, and salt. They suit a roast dinner, a sandwich, or a soup bowl equally well.
sourceNotes: Plain white bread rolls follow the same formula as any lean bread: flour, water, yeast, salt. The technique appears in standard British breadmaking manuals throughout the twentieth century. Elizabeth David, English Bread and Yeast Cookery (1977), documents basic roll shapes and their relationship to the loaf formula.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool fully, seal in a bag, and freeze. Refresh from frozen in a 200°C oven for 8 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Shape and refrigerate overnight. Bake from cold, adding 4 to 5 minutes.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the high heat sets the crust quickly
baking.steamMethod: water tray on the oven floor for the first 10 minutes

content.text: White bread rolls are a lean dough: just flour, water, yeast, and salt, with no fat or enrichment. They bake up with a thin, crackly crust and an airy, slightly chewy crumb. The same dough that makes a good tin loaf makes a better roll in terms of crust-to-crumb ratio, because of the greater surface area per piece.

Mix the flour, yeast, and salt in a large bowl. Add the water and bring together into a rough dough. Knead for 10 minutes by hand until smooth and elastic. A 65% hydration dough is soft and slightly sticky at first; it will clear the bowl and become noticeably easier to handle after 5 minutes.
Shape into a ball, return to the bowl, cover, and leave for 1 hour. The dough should double in volume.

Turn the dough out and divide into 10 pieces, about 83 g each. Shape each into a tight round using a cupped hand on the work surface. Place on a lightly floured baking tray and cover loosely. Prove for 45 minutes until noticeably puffy. The rolls should hold a finger impression gently and not spring all the way back.

Place a small tray of water in the oven and heat to 220°C fan. Dust the rolls with flour and bake for 18 to 20 minutes. They are done when the tops are a deep gold and the rolls sound hollow when tapped on the base. Cool on a wire rack.
